I find the Japanese culture very different than the Chinese culture. We enjoy yum cha whereas they enjoy drinking green tea at bars, soaking up the atmosphere rather than at home. My Japanese friend Keita is a Scorpio while his girlfriend is a Gemini. He's faithful to his girlfriend and would never cheat on her. When he broke up with his ex, he was driven over the edge. Even when a stranger talked to him, there was a definite edge of hostility to his voice. But as soon as he had a new relationship target, it took the edge of his emotional upset. His ex is a swimming champion who far outshines us in any swimming styles. She's tall and lean who definitely has the edge over lots of swimmers. Recently, Keita was in a brawl with a guy who was hitting on his girlfriend. The police showed up later and assigned jealousy as the motive of the brawl. Last Sunday, they dined in a restaurant in a hotel which is midway between his place and the post office. They stood in line outside the restaurant for almost an hour. The line was so huge that it stretched from the counter through the lobby area and out the main entrance of the hotel. A stout guy tried to cut in line but was caught. We met at a midway point between his place and mine. Then he took me to the shuttle bus bound for his place. A group of people was in the way near the bus stop so he said "Excuse me." The queue was so big, stretching down the road to the furthest minibus stop. We lined up for half an hour until three shuttle buses came along at once. Meanwhile, some people mistook this long line for the line for other places. A couple came up to us and asked "Are you in the line for Azura?" as they wanna make sure that it's the right line. A burly man pushed in midway through the queue. Later, a slender guy jumped the line. The lady behind him said "Excuse me. The end of the line is back there." A woman stood beside a guy who was midway through the line. While pretending to look at her phone, she edged furtively behind him who didn't realize she was there. Some people might have noticed it but ignored it. Keita seemed on edge. It turns out he has a job interview next Thursday. Never has he felt so on edge before an interview. His elder brother is currently a head chef at a five-star hotel and feels he's found his niche. His younger brother has also found his niche as a professor, having worked as a researcher for three years. He sometimes has a snooze at lunchtime because he has so much work to do that he feels drained. He usually naps on the sofa by the fireplace or at his desk when everybody else's eating lunch outside. He once brought his work along to the library where I saw him doze off at the table. Having a doze especially in front of the fire is a marvelous way to replenish your energy though. His eldest brother has just completed his PhD at Peking University and is on the way to becoming a professor. China is on the way to becoming the world's strongest economy. His elder sister is robust and healthy. She does lots of strength and conditioning exercises in her robust sneakers. After her gym session, she usually drinks a cup of robust coffee and a glass or robust wine. She lives in Japan where the economy and the political system are robust. His younger sister has opened an antique shop which made a great impression on me. There's an exquisite antique which engages my interest. I'll bet the store engages lots of passers-by. I formed a favorable impression of the shop. Keita's uncle has recently passed away at the age of ninety nine. I offer my sincere condolences to his uncle's family. replies.msg: I find the Japanese culture very different to our culture. We enjoy yum cha whereas they enjoy drinking green tea at bars, soaking up the atmosphere rather than at home. My Japanese friend Isaac is a Leo while his girlfriend is a Pisces. He's faithful to her and would never cheat on her. When he broke up with his ex, he was pushed over the edge. Even when a stranger talked to him, there was an edge in his voice. But as soon as he had a new relationship target, it took the edge of his emotional upset. His ex is a swimming champion who easily outshines us in any swimming styles. She's tall and shapely who certainly has an edge over lots of swimmers. Recently, Isaac got into a scuffle with a guy who was hitting on his girlfriend. The police showed up later and jealousy was assigned as the motive for the scuffle. Last Saturday, they dined in a restaurant in a hotel which is midway between the supermarket and the post office. The queue was so huge that it stretched from the counter through the lobby area and out the main entrance of the hotel. A stout man tried to cut in line but was caught. We met at a midway point between his place and mine then he took me to the shuttle bus bound for his place. A group of people was in the way near the bus stop so he said "Excuse me." The line was so big, stretching down the road to the furthest minibus stop. We queued up for half an hour until three shuttle buses came along at once. In the meantime, some people mistook this long queue for the queue for other places. A couple came up and asked "Are you in the queue for Cluny Park?" as they wanna be sure that it's the right queue. A burly guy pushed in at the tail of the queue. Later, a slim man jumped the queue. The lady behind him said "Excuse me. The end of the queue is back there." A svelte woman stood beside a slender guy who was midway through the line. While pretending to look at her phone, she edged furtively behind him who didn't realize she was there. Some people might have noticed it but ignored it. Isaac seemed on edge. It turns out he has a job interview next week. Never has he felt so on edge before an interview. His elder sister is currently a head chef at a four-star hotel and feels she's found her niche. His younger sister has also found her niche as a professor, having worked as a researcher for four years. She sometimes has a doze at lunchtime because she has so much work to do that she feels drained. She usually snoozes on the sofa by the fire or ather desk when everyone else's eating lunch outside.  She once brought her work along with her to the library where she dozed off at the table. Taking a nap especially by the fire is a marvelous way to recharge your energy though. His eldest sister has just completed her PhD at Peking University and is on the way to becoming a professor. China is on the way to becoming the world's strongest economy. His elder brother is healthy and robust. He does a lot of strength and conditioning exercises in his robust sneakers. After his gym sessions, he usually drinks a cup of robust coffee and a glass of robust wine. He lives in Japan where the political system and the economy are robust. His younger brother has opened an antique store lately which created a favorable impression on me. There's an exquisite antique which engages my attention. I bet you that the shop engages lots of passers-by. I formed a great impression of the shop. Hos uncle has recently passed away at the age of ninety eight. I sent my condolences to his uncle's family. His friend Jeremy said "My condolences on the loss of your uncle." His mom also offered her sincere condolences to his family. His dad wrote "Our condolences go to his family." Isaac hadn't heard from his cousin Leonardo for years until last Tuesday when he received a call from him. They chatted with exquisite delight. Leonardo told Isaac that there was a bully in his class so Isaac told him not to engage with him. Just stay out of his way as much as possible.

Isaac lives in the most exclusive and expensive area in Tokyo and owns many houses. Unlike Floyd, he's never ostentatious though extremely wealthy. You can find out how ostentatious Floyd's lifestyle is on YouTube, such as his house and car collections. He always wears ostentatious jewelry, such as gold bracelets, rings and silver necklaces. He loves driving flashy cars and wearing flashy clothes. Do you remember the incident in Langham Place where the escalator stopped and reversed direction at a speed three times faster than normal, causing people to fall and pile up at the base of the escalator? People were screaming as they tumbled down the escalator. Some people managed to grab the handrail and steady themselves. The incident occasioned a good deal of worry. When you were in secondary school, were there any subjects which you weren't interested in so you didn't engage much in the classes? I remember I didnt really engage in geography classes. I was often daydreaming. Nowadays, many children learn piano, violin, painting, dancing, judo and Japanese. I honestly don't know how they juggle these activities and schoolwork. Some of their parents have to go to work so they find it hard to juggle children with a career. Perhaps they wanna kindle their kids' interest and see which one they can cultivate an interest in or have a talent for. Sometimes I wonder if the children are truly enjoying their activities. Isaac loves video games. U remember him playing Counterstrike in which he used a gun to single-handedly engage ninety nine enemies who only had knives. I'm reading a book called Parallel Worlds written by physicist Kaku. When he was explaining the theories on a television show, promoting the book, it captured my attention. It's a fascinating book which explores the science of time travel, parallel universes, eleven dimensional hyperspace and wormholes, subjects that engage numerous readers' interest around the world. I find the subject of black holes most engaging. It was an international bestseller for many months.

研究指出，蓋茨擁有農田最多的州是在路易斯安那州，持有69,071英畝；阿肯色州持有47,927英畝；內布拉斯加州擁有20,588英畝；在美國各地購買了24.2萬英畝的耕地，這足以使他成為美國最大的私人農田所有者。
據報，蓋茨的私人投資公司Cascade Investments直接或通過第三方實體持有土地。雖然一位IT(資訊科技)業界的億萬富翁成為美國最大的農戶，有點令人感到驚訝，但這其實不是蓋茨第一次涉足農業領域。2008年，其基金會就宣布提供3.06億美元贈款，在非洲及南亞的小農中，促進高產及可持續的農業。目前還不完全清楚蓋茨將如何利用農田，也不清楚是否有土地被預留用於保護，但目前情況表明，這些土地的使用方式符合蓋茨基金會的價值觀。
